report_name: Cybersecurity Vendor Trellix Confirms Breach of Source Code Repository

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Actors Targeting Trellix
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actors responsible for the unauthorized access and breach of a portion of Trellix's internal source code repository.
threat_actors.goals: Vulnerability discovery in Trellix products, development of detection bypasses, and potential future supply chain attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Considered the most likely initial access method, where attackers used compromised developer credentials or a stolen access token to gain unauthorized access to the source code repository.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Unauthorized actors accessed and exfiltrated a portion of Trellix's internal source code repository, which is a form of data collection from cloud storage.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a potential method for gaining access, where attackers could have stolen a Personal Access Token (PAT) for a source code management platform like GitHub or GitLab.

identities.name: Trellix
identities.description: A cybersecurity vendor formed from the merger of McAfee Enterprise and FireEye, which confirmed a security breach involving unauthorized access to its internal source code repository.

identities.name: McAfee Enterprise
identities.description: Mentioned as one of the companies that merged to form Trellix.

identities.name: FireEye
identities.description: Mentioned as one of the companies that merged to form Trellix.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Mentioned as another security-focused company that has faced a similar source code repository breach.

identities.name: Okta
identities.description: Mentioned as another security-focused company that has faced a similar source code repository breach.

identities.name: LastPass
identities.description: Mentioned as another security-focused company that has faced a similar source code repository breach.

indicators.name: Suspicious Git Clone with Embedded Credentials
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Git Clone with Embedded Credentials
id: 1e9a4c2b-8f7d-4b6c-9a3e-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects git clone commands that include credentials directly in the URL, which can indicate credential exposure or misuse.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/trellix-confirms-source-code-breach.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1528
  - attack.t1552.001
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Some automated scripts or CI/CD systems might use this method, although it is not a recommended practice.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\git.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'clone'
      - 'https://'
      - '@'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects git clone commands that include credentials (like a Personal Access Token) directly in the URL, a potential vector for the Trellix breach.

indicators.name: Anomalous Source Code Repository Cloning
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Source Code Repository Cloning
id: 5a6b7c8d-9e0f-4a1b-8c2d-3e4f5a6b7c8d
status: experimental
description: Detects cloning of source code repositories from audit logs, which could be indicative of unauthorized data exfiltration. This rule should be tuned to filter for anomalous activity, such as cloning from new or untrusted locations or by unusual users.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/trellix-confirms-source-code-breach.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate developer activity. This rule requires baselining and correlation with other signals (e.g., source IP geolocation) to be effective.
level: low
logsource:
  category: audit
detection:
  selection:
    keywords:
      - 'repo.clone' # GitHub audit log event for cloning
      - 'project.git_access' # GitLab audit log event for cloning/fetching
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects source code repository cloning events in audit logs, which was the core action in the Trellix breach.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Git Clone with Embedded Credentials
